corpus.title: Xi, Kim pledge to carry forward traditional China-DPRK friendship
corpus.signal_text:
Xi, Kim pledge to carry forward traditional China-DPRK friendship

Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee and Chinese president, and Kim Jong Un, general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea (WPK) and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), agreed on Tuesday that the two countries should carry forward their great traditional friendship from generation to generation.

corpus.title: Xi Jinping's remarks on China-DPRK relations
corpus.signal_text:
Xi Jinping's remarks on China-DPRK relations

At the invitation of Kim Jong Un, general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and Chinese president, arrived in Pyongyang on Monday for a state visit to the DPRK.

corpus.title: Xi: Important consensus reached on China-DPRK ties in new era
corpus.signal_text:
Xi: Important consensus reached on China-DPRK ties in new era

Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and Chinese president, said on Tuesday that he reached important consensus with General Secretary Kim Jong Un on developing relations between China and the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) in the new era.

corpus.title: Xi returns to Beijing after state visit to DPRK
corpus.signal_text:
Xi returns to Beijing after state visit to DPRK

Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee and Chinese president, returned to Beijing on Tuesday afternoon after concluding his state visit to the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK).

corpus.title: To denuclearize North Korea, Xi must make Kim an offer he cannot refuse
corpus.signal_text:
To denuclearize North Korea, Xi must make Kim an offer he cannot refuse

Following his recent meetings with President Donald Trump and President Vladimir Putin, Chinese President Xi Jinping traveled to Pyongyang on 8-9 June, his first visit in nearly seven years. corpus.title: Stop watching Kim Ju Ae and start watching North Korea-China ties
corpus.signal_text:
Stop watching Kim Ju Ae and start watching North Korea-China ties

Chinese President Xi Jinping’s visit to North Korea in June 2026 was no mere diplomatic ceremony. Timed to the 65th anniversary of the North Korea-China Treaty of Friendship, Cooperation and Mutual Assistance, the trip revealed both the present and future of relations between the two countries. The Chinese leader’s long-awaited visit to Pyongyang aims to counter Russian influence.

corpus.title: US adversaries China, North Korea strengthening ties as Xi, Kim set to begin talks
corpus.signal_text:
US adversaries China, North Korea strengthening ties as Xi, Kim set to begin talks

Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a rare state visit, set to meet with North Korea's Kim Jong Un as both nations seek to restore their traditional alliance.

corpus.title: North Korea says Chinese president’s visit produced ‘far-reaching blueprint’ for ties
corpus.signal_text:
North Korea says Chinese president’s visit produced ‘far-reaching blueprint’ for ties

The leaders of North Korea and China adopted a “far-reaching blueprint” for bilateral ties during Xi Jinping’s recent visit to Pyongyang, the state-run Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) said on Wednesday. China’s president made a rare visit to diplomatically isolated North Korea on Monday after hosting a series of world leaders, including US President Donald […]

corpus.title: Chinese leader Xi lands in North Korea for rare visit
corpus.signal_text:
Chinese leader Xi lands in North Korea for rare visit

China’s President Xi Jinping hailed an “invincible friendship” with Pyongyang on arrival in North Korea on Monday, his first trip abroad this year after hosting back-to-back summits in Beijing. China, Washington’s chief geopolitical rival, has been North Korea’s main trading partner by far for decades and a key source of diplomatic and economic support for […]

corpus.title: Seoul says denuclearization remains goal despite absence from Xi-Kim summit

Seoul says denuclearization remains goal despite absence from Xi-Kim summit

South Korea has reaffirmed its commitment to the denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula, after North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and Chinese President Xi Jinping omitted mention of the DPRK’s nuclear program at their summit this week. corpus.title: Why Was Xi Jinping in North Korea?
corpus.signal_text:
Why Was Xi Jinping in North Korea?

The seven-year gap since Xi’s previous visit to North Korea suggests a cooling in bilateral relations. What does it mean that he made the trip to Pyongyang at last?
